items.title: Oil prices jump 4% as Rubio says Iran 'not serious' about peace talks
items.description: Oil prices rose as hopes for a ceasefire in the Middle East faded.

items.title: Citizens United provision allowing foreign campaign contributions draws Democratic bill
items.description: Rep. Jamie Raskin, D-Md., and Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse, D-R.I., want to establish foreign ownership thresholds for companies that spend on campaigns.

items.title: Kalshi launches election hub for prediction markets ahead of midterms
items.description: The company said the page gives viewers of its event contracts a central place to see speculators' outlook for the midterm elections.

items.title: Zelenskyy ousts Ukraine’s army chief as protests expose wartime power struggle
items.description: The reshuffle comes after almost a week of nationwide unrest and represents one of the biggest political crises since Russia's full-scale invasion in 2022.

items.title: Trump plans generic drug tariffs from 2028 with two-year delay testing U.S. onshoring push
items.description: The latest tariff salvo underscored Trump's goal of reshoring low-cost drug production, but displacing established suppliers like India won't be easy.

items.title: Japan exports and imports in June grow at fastest pace since November 2022, beating estimates
items.description: Exports climbed 19.3%, while imports spiked 25.4% compared to June last year.

items.title: Trump's push for American-made AI chips hits TSMC's margins
items.description: The Taiwan-based chipmaker has announced $200 billion in investment into U.S. manufacturing since Trump returned to power in 2025.

items.title: India steps up energy shift as costs and tariff pressures mount — oil blending push sparks backlash
items.description: India is banking on alternative fuels as it prioritizes energy security amid high oil prices and supply constraints.

items.title: Wistron shares surge in Taiwan after opening of Texas AI superchip plant to supply Nvidia
items.description: Taiwan’s Wistron Corporation opens first U.S. facility in Fort Worth, Texas.

items.title: These Japanese AI winners don’t make chips. They make toilets, glass fiber and seasoning
items.description: A toilet maker, a glass fiber manufacturer and the maker of MSG have in Japan have become major beneficiaries of the AI boom.

items.title: $100M New Jersey deli fraudster James Patten sentenced to 21 months in prison
items.description: The notorious New Jersey deli fraud case led to losses of more than $5 million for victims that included two U.S. universities.

items.title: Mamdani says New York lacks authority to arrest Netanyahu, calls him ‘war criminal’
items.description: Israel's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, is the target of an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Court related to the war on Gaza.

items.title: Sony’s 'ironic' PlayStation disc decision upends gamer conventions and threatens a $7 billion resale market
items.description: In 2013, Sony tweaked Xbox by showing how easy it was to share a PlayStation disc. Now, gamers say Sony has become exactly what it mocked.

items.title: Nike to cut off thousands of online distributors in China, restructure digital footprint
items.description: Nike is streamlining its online business in China as the company looks to stabilize pricing and branding and get back to growth.

items.title: Capital One earnings were good enough but didn't answer the big question hanging over the stock
items.description: Investors want to know when Capital One will begin to see more tangible benefits from its Discover and Brex deals.

items.title: The company behind Post-it notes has found an unlikely role in the AI buildout
items.description: 3M CEO Bill Brown said its optical connectivity technology can cut fiber installation time by up to 85% as hyperscalers build AI data centers.

items.title: Prediction markets and their opponents are spending more to influence Congress in 2026
items.description: Kalshi and groups representing the casino and gaming industry and online sports betting apps, all have boosted their lobbying spending in 2026.

items.title: The Fed rang the alarm about Anthropic's Mythos AI model — but had to go months without it
items.description: The central bank didn't have access to Claude Mythos Preview as of mid-July, even as other institutions were racing to patch their vulnerabilities.

items.title: Super Micro surges 15% on new order and margin disclosure after SpaceX announcement
items.description: Many server makers are seeing faster growth as companies race to deploy artificial intelligence servers.

items.title: RFK Jr. says cyclospora outbreak is 'under control'
items.description: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday said that the ongoing outbreak of cyclosporiasis is "under control."
